part.part_title: Special Populations and Life Course Approach

learning_objectives.text: Define key concepts in occupational health and safety including work-related diseases, injuries, and hazards
Describe the global burden of occupational diseases and injuries across different regions and economic contexts
Identify major categories of workplace hazards: chemical, physical, biological, ergonomic, and psychosocial
Explain the hierarchy of controls for occupational risk management
List key international labor standards and conventions related to worker health and safety
Compare occupational health challenges in formal versus informal economic sectors
Explain how industrialization and economic development affect occupational health patterns
Describe the relationship between occupational health and broader public health outcomes
Summarize the role of occupational health services in health system strengthening
Apply exposure assessment principles to evaluate workplace health risks
Design workplace health and safety intervention programs for specific occupational settings
Develop occupational health surveillance systems appropriate for different economic contexts
Implement occupational disease prevention strategies using evidence-based approaches
Analyze the effectiveness of different occupational health intervention models
Evaluate occupational health surveillance data to identify trends and priorities
Assess the impact of globalization and supply chains on worker health across countries
Compare occupational health approaches between developed and developing countries
Integrate occupational health considerations into broader health system planning
Develop comprehensive occupational health policies that address formal and informal workers
Create multi-stakeholder approaches to improving workplace health and safety
Design occupational health research studies addressing global health priorities
Critically assess the effectiveness of international labor standards implementation
Evaluate the cost-effectiveness of occupational health interventions
Judge the quality and utility of occupational health surveillance systems
Appraise occupational health policies for their potential to reduce health inequities

case_studies.title: Brazil's National Program for Silicosis Elimination
key_themes: Silicosis prevention in mining and construction
learning_objectives: Analyze the effectiveness of national silicosis prevention programs
Evaluate multi-sectoral approaches to occupational disease prevention

case_studies.title: Singapore's Construction Safety Transformation
learning_objectives: Assess the role of technology in workplace injury prevention
Evaluate economic incentive mechanisms for occupational safety

case_studies.title: Ethiopia's Integrated Occupational Health Services
learning_objectives: Analyze integration models for occupational health in primary healthcare
Evaluate occupational health service delivery in low-resource settings

case_studies.title: India's Informal Sector Worker Health Initiative
key_themes: Street vendor and domestic worker health
learning_objectives: Design occupational health interventions for informal sector workers
Evaluate community-based approaches to worker health protection

items.question: According to the hierarchy of controls in occupational health, which approach should be prioritized first?
items.explanation: The hierarchy of controls prioritizes elimination as the most effective control method, followed by substitution, engineering controls, administrative controls, and personal protective equipment.

items.question: Which international organization sets global labor standards including occupational health and safety?
items.explanation: The ILO is the specialized UN agency responsible for setting international labor standards, including conventions on occupational health and safety.

items.question: What proportion of the global workforce is estimated to be employed in the informal economy?
items.explanation: The ILO estimates that approximately 60% of the global workforce is employed in the informal economy, presenting significant challenges for occupational health protection.

items.question: Describe the key components of a workplace health surveillance system and explain how it differs from general health surveillance.
items.learning_objective: Apply surveillance principles to occupational health contexts

items.question: Explain how globalization and international supply chains create both challenges and opportunities for protecting worker health globally.
items.learning_objective: Analyze the impact of globalization on occupational health

items.title: Developing an Occupational Health Program for a Textile Factory
items.scenario: You are tasked with developing an occupational health program for a textile manufacturing facility in Bangladesh employing 2,000 workers, primarily young women. The facility produces garments for international brands.
tasks: Identify key occupational health hazards in textile manufacturing
Design a health surveillance program appropriate for this setting
Develop strategies to address gender-specific occupational health risks
Create a plan for integrating occupational health with local healthcare services
learning_objectives: Integrate occupational health with health systems
